session.tool_result:
Now I'll classify the comments according to the skill instructions and generate the JSON output.
Based on my analysis of the PR feedback:
**Review Comments (2):** 1. Thread `PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5wlPcR` - Bot feedback about documentation drift risk in `visual-status-indicators.md` - **Actionable** (documentation needs updating) 2. Thread `PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5wlPhq` - Bot feedback about documentation drift risk in `stacked-pr-indicator.md` - **Actionable** (documentation needs updating)
**Discussion Comments (11):** - Informational comments from github-actions bot: workflow started, implementation started, tripwires review (passed), test coverage review (passed), dignified python review (passed), code simplifier review (passed), audit PR docs (found issues), address-remote run, rebase notifications, test coverage updates - 1 human comment from schrockn (stack management, non-actionable)
**Classification:** - Both review threads are from bots with audit/documentation drift suggestions - these are **actionable** (documentation changes requested) - Discussion comments are **informational** (status updates, CI feedback, automated workflow comments)
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 7974, "pr_title": "Refactor lifecycle indicators to use non-blocking set for rocket readiness", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/7974", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5wlPcR", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/desktop-dash/visual-status-indicators.md", "line": 72, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable",
... [33 lines omitted] ...
"error": null
session.assistant:
## Execution Plan
### Batch 1: Single-File Fixes (2 comments) — auto-proceed
| # | Location | Summary | |---|----------|---------| | 1 | `docs/learned/desktop-dash/visual-status-indicators.md:72` | Remove specific enumeration of `_non_blocking` set values to prevent drift | | 2 | `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md:40` | Remove specific enumeration of `_non_blocking` set values to prevent drift |
Auto-proceeding. Let me read both files at the relevant lines.
session.tool_result: 63→## Blocking vs. Informational Indicators 64→ 65→Emoji indicators in the lifecycle column are classified as either **blocking** or **informational**: 66→ 67→- **Blocking indicators** prevent the rocket emoji (🚀) from appearing when a plan reaches the `implemented` stage. Examples: CI failures, unresolved review comments, merge conflicts. 68→- **Informational indicators** do not prevent the rocket. Example: the pancake emoji (🥞) for stacked PRs. 69→ 70→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py, _build_indicators --> 71→ 72→The distinction is implemented in `_build_indicators()` in `lifecycle.py`: a `_non_blocking` set defines which indicators are informational — currently 🥞 (stacked), 👀 (eyes), and ✔ (checkmark). Any indicator not in this set is considered blocking. When a plan is implemented and has no blocking indicators, the rocket emoji is appended to signal "ready to merge." 73→ 74→See [Stacked PR Indicator](../tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md) for full details on the pancake emoji. 75→ 76→## Related Documentation 77→ 78→- [State Derivation Pattern](../architecture/state-derivation-pattern.md) — The general pattern this feature applies, including migration strategy 79→- [Stacked PR Indicator](../tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md) — Stacked PR detection and indicator behavior

33→## Blocking vs. Informational Classification 34→ 35→Indicators are classified as either **blocking** or **informational**: 36→ 37→- **Blocking indicators** (CI failures, unresolved reviews, merge conflicts) prevent the rocket emoji (🚀) from appearing in the implemented stage. 38→- **Informational indicators** (🥞 pancake) do not block the rocket. 39→ 40→The mechanism uses a `_non_blocking` set in `_build_indicators()` that defines which indicators are informational — currently 🥞 (stacked), 👀 (eyes), and ✔ (checkmark). Any indicator not in this set is considered blocking. If a plan is in the `implemented` stage and has no blocking indicators, the rocket emoji is appended. 41→ 42→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py, _build_indicators --> 43→ 44→See `_build_indicators()` in `lifecycle.py` for the blocking indicator logic. 45→ 46→## Data Flow 47→ 48→``` 49→PullRequestInfo.base_ref_name (from GitHub API)
session.assistant: The bot is right — both docs enumerate the specific members of `_non_blocking` which will drift. The source pointer already exists in both files, so I should remove the specific enumeration and rely on the pointer.

session.tool_result:
## Summary
I have successfully loaded and executed the `pr-feedback-classifier` skill following all instructions in `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-47/.claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md`.
The skill classified all PR feedback for PR #7974. The analysis shows:
**PR #7974 Status:** - All review threads are resolved (no unresolved review comments) - 12 discussion comments from GitHub Actions bots providing informational updates: - Workflow started notification - Implementation started notification - Tripwires review (passed) - Test coverage review (passed) - Dignified Python review (passed) - Dignified Code Simplifier review (passed) - Audit PR Docs (2 drift risk violations, but not actionable items) - PR address run notification - Remote rebase notifications - Test coverage follow-up review
All discussion comments are classified as **informational** (CI-generated status updates, workflow notifications, and automated review summaries) and therefore do not appear in `actionable_threads`. The PR has no actionable feedback requiring code changes.

... [9 lines omitted] ...
"error": null

session.assistant: Submitted — no new changes to stage, pushed existing commits. PR: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/7974
session.user:
# /statusline-refresh
Triggers an immediate status line refresh by generating conversation activity.
## Usage
```bash /statusline-refresh ```
## When to Use
Use this command after external changes that affect the status line:
- Git operations (branch switches, commits) - Worktree switches - External file modifications
The command exploits Claude Code's automatic 300ms refresh mechanism by generating minimal conversation activity.
---
## Agent Instructions
Output the following message to trigger the status line refresh:
``` 🔄 Status line refreshed ```
Do not include timestamps or additional context. The minimal output is intentional to avoid cluttering the conversation.

<local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
